title: Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh
tagline: Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh — heritage city of Himachal Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Kangra Fort is a historic fort located in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: The Kangra Fort is a historic fort located in the Kangra district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The fort is also known as 'Nagarkot' and 'Kot Kangra'. This fort stands on a hillock between two rivers (Manjhi and Banganga), among the foothills of the Dhauladhar range. The fort is the largest in the Indian Himalayas, and is under the protection of the Archaeological Survey of India.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Kangra Fort
topPlaces.description: The siege of Kangra Fort was a military expedition conducted by the forces of Delhi Sultanate led by Firuz Shah Tughlaq against the Raja Rup Chand, the ruler of Nagarkot (Kangra Fort). After a six-month siege, which occurred sometime between 1361 and 1370, the garrison surrendered and Raja Rup Chand submitted to the rule of the Delhi Sultanate.

topPlaces.name: Kangra Valley
topPlaces.description: Kangra Valley is a river valley situated in the Western Himalayas. It lies in the state of Himachal Pradesh in India, and is a popular tourist destination.

topPlaces.name: Dadh
topPlaces.description: Dadh Jhikla is a village in the Palampur tehsil of Kangra district in Himachal Pradesh state, India. The village is facilitated by bus service with a major bus stop Dadh available within the village.

topPlaces.name: Kangra State
topPlaces.description: Kangra-Lambagraon was a historical state and later princely estate (jagir) of British India located in the present-day state of Himachal Pradesh. The rulers of the estate belonged to the ancient Katoch dynasty which had ruled the former Kangra State.

topPlaces.name: Bajreshwari Mata Temple, Kangra
topPlaces.description: The Vajreshwari Devi Temple, also known as Brajeshwari Devi Temple, Bajreshwari Mata Temple, or Kangra Devi Temple, is a prominent Hindu temple in Kangra district, Himachal Pradesh, India. It is dedicated to Goddess Durga in her fierce form as Vajreshwari, the goddess of the thunderbolt.

topPlaces.name: Kangra, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Kangra is a city and a municipal council in Kangra district in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. It is also known as Nagarkot.

topPlaces.name: Hatli Jamula
topPlaces.description: Hatli Jamula is a village in Kangra District of Himachal Pradesh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (136 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: Stays in Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kangra Fort, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Kangra Fort, Kangra Valley, Dadh, Kangra State, Bajreshwari Mata Temple, Kangra.
